dominants.dominant_desc:
Play safe.
Focus on leveling up to level 4.
Rotate clearing minion at exp and mid lane. 
Buy cocolate boots. 
Then proceed with the sky piercer item.

Pro tips:
1.stay aware with exp enemy position when rotating clear minion wave at mid to exp lane. 
2.SKY PIERCER FIRST. because chances of enemy with low hp is higher at early game. So use the early gane wisely to get more sky piercer stack.

1.after already level 4, you can PLAY a little bit more AGRESSIVE but still rotate clear own exp lane.
2.Try going to gold lane, to support the mm and tank.Why? Are you surprised?... Well the enemy marksmen were also surprised. (better if you can) (if cannot stay calm, focused clear wave and buy items) 
3.Try invade enemy creeps.
4.THEN PROCEED BUY BLADE OF HEPTASEAS

If you are already in the late game. 
Make sure to always clear the minions on each lane (lane pressure method on enemies)

If your damage doesn't Makes an impact on the late game. You can turn those items into defensive items. So your teamates will have more thick lives to absorb enemy damage at the end of the game.

emblems.emblem_desc:
Vitality was chosen because chou at the beginning of the game was indeed a bit sluggish. So with vitality he has an extra 225 max hp at the beginning of the game to survive at exp lane even if the enemy in that lane is more than one. 

Seasoned hunter was chosen because it can speed up chou to invade creep enemies to level it up quickly at the same time this perk can help speed up the capture of turtles or even lords.

The concussive blast was chosen because it is able to clear lanes quickly because the passive wave of this talent releases the magic around it. It is also an extra damage for chou when struggling in battle.

equips.equip_desc:
When using this build. Focus on sky piercer first,Because with the chou emblem tank, he was a bit tanky at the beginning of the game. So, better focus on damage first. He can help hyper to start the battle when invading enemy creeps. 

Tips:
1.Focus on sky piercer first. Try to get stacked as many as possible at early game.
2.buy boots (depends on the enemy line up) (physical or magical) 
3.buy blade of heptaseas for better penetration to the enemy. 
4.buy malefic gun. Why?. Because malefic gun have passive   that allows chou to move faster. At the same time, malefic guns can be used on enemy tanks more efficiently compared to malefic roar. (more focus on speed, attack, penetration).
5.By buying hunter strike, chou can move and clear lane even faster. Thanks for the thanks to the passive hunter strike which adds movement speed when hitting 5 times on the same creep. As well as can help hyper lord or turtle faster. You can catch remaining enemy faster while your hp was quite tanky. 
4. For the highest damage, we will buy a blade of dispair. You can buy this item early if you already dominate your exp lane. With this item, enemies below 20% hp will be easily to eliminate.

hero.hero_overview: This hero has a lot of damage thanks to his passivity which has high penetration. He also has a lot of mobility where skill 1 has 3 dashes and skill 2 has cc and is able to dash making him a hero who has good mobility when entering and exiting any intense battle.
hero.hero_strength:
Strengths of this hero:
1. has great damage (thanks to its passive)
2. It has good mobility (so it can make quick rotations when needed) 
3. He is also able to control the battle thanks to his skill 1 where the last skill can bounce the enemies around him for a while for his group to activate the skill. At the same time can help friends from certain cc skills.
4.This hero is also a bit tanky. If this hero makes a semi defense item. He was able to survive in a tough war.
hero.hero_weakness:
Weaknesses of this hero
1. If you do full damage. The probability of dying a lot is high.this hero should preferably buy a defense item either physical or magical. 
2. This hero is a single target hero. Where skill 3 is only able to kick one enemy hero towards a friend.
3. This hero is difficult when he has to fight 1vs 2. For example sun.

emblems.emblem_desc: I chose this Emblem set to enhance my early-game poke and lane pressure. The Concussive Blast talent synergizes well with heroes who frequently engage in basic attacks or close-range combos, offering bonus AoE magic damage that scales with max HP. This helps in both zoning enemies and assisting in team fights. It's especially effective for tanky or semi-tank heroes that stay close to enemies, like Chou or Atlas, providing additional damage without sacrificing durability.

equips.equip_desc:
The key equipment in this build is Thunder Belt, Endless Battle, and Dominance Ice.

Thunder Belt is core because it synergizes with skill-based heroes like Chou, providing true damage, cooldown reduction, and extra sustain from HP and mana regen. Endless Battle enhances Chou's combo burst by giving additional true damage after using a skill, along with lifesteal and movement speed to keep up with targets or escape. Dominance Ice plays a crucial role in team fights by reducing enemy attack speed and healing, making it highly effective against heroes with high sustain or fast basic attacks.

These three items define the balance of offense, utility, and survivability that this build is aiming for.
pure physical penetrations also can swap BoH to Hunter strike

hero.hero_overview: Chou is a highly mobile fighter known for his exceptional crowd control and strong pick-off potential. His skills allow him to dash through enemies, knock them up, and isolate key targets with his ultimate. He is often used to disable and eliminate enemy backline heroes, especially squishy damage dealers. Chou's versatility makes him viable as both a durable tanky roamer or a high-damage sidelaner.
hero.hero_strength: Chou excels in mobility and crowd control, making him a strong initiator and duelist. With powerful control effects from his skills and ultimate, he can easily isolate and eliminate high-value targets, especially backline mages and marksmen. Chou's second skill grants brief control immunity and a shield, helping him dodge critical enemy skills. He can be played as a tanky roamer or damage-dealing laner, and his skill-based mechanics give him high outplay potential in the hands of experienced players.
hero.hero_weakness: Chou, despite his strengths, has several weaknesses that players must manage carefully. He is highly skill-dependent, meaning poor timing or misused abilities can render him ineffective in fights. Crowd control immunity is brief and requires precise use, making him vulnerable if mistimed. His damage output is low without proper items or combos, especially in a tank build. Chou also struggles against heroes with strong mobility or blink skills, as they can escape his combos. Additionally, his ultimate can be easily canceled or interrupted if not used carefully, particularly when facing heroes with instant CC or Purify. Proper timing, positioning, and decision-making are crucial to maximize his impact.

hero.hero_overview:
Chou Overview
Role: Fighter / Tank
Specialty: Charge / Control
Lane: Usually played as an EXP Laner, Roamer, or sometimes Jungler (though not common).
hero.hero_strength:
Great Crowd Control: Can knock up and suppress enemies.

High Mobility: Hard to catch thanks to dashes and CC immunity.

Anti-Carry: Excellent at picking off squishy heroes.

Versatile: Can play as fighter, tank, or roamer.

High Skill Ceiling: Big outplay and combo potential.
hero.hero_weakness:
Skill Dependent: Needs precise timing and combos to be effective.

Hard to Master: High learning curve, especially for advanced plays.

Item Dependent (as Fighter): Needs farm to deal damage.

Easily Countered When Locked Down: If caught in CC before using Shunpo.

Not Ideal in 5v5 Brawls: Better at picking off targets than full team fights.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Chou synergizes well with Kaja due to their strong pick-off and crowd control abilities. Kaja's Divine Judgment can pull an enemy into Chou's Jeet Kune Do + Way of the Dragon combo, ensuring the target gets locked down and eliminated quickly. This duo excels at isolating key enemies and disrupting the enemy team.

counters.counter_desc: Chou is strong against Aldous because he can use Way of the Dragon to stop Aldous mid-air when he uses Chase Fate (Ultimate). This prevents Aldous from landing his powerful punch and escaping fights.

dominants.dominant_desc: Focus on clearing minion waves while avoiding unnecessary fights. Use Jeet Kune Do (1st skill) to poke enemies and establish lane control. Prioritize reaching level 4 to unlock The Way of the Dragon (Ultimate) for potential solo kills or rotations.

Once you reach level 4, look for opportunities to eliminate key enemies using Chou's ultimate. Push your lane, then rotate to mid or help secure the Turtle. Build Blade of Heptaseas for extra burst damage and Endless Battle for sustain. Play aggressively but avoid overextending without backup.

In the late game, focus on split pushing or flanking in team fights. Use Chou's ultimate to isolate and eliminate priority targets like enemy mages or marksmen. Build Malefic Roar to counter enemy armor and Immortality for survivability. Always check the map before engaging, and avoid unnecessary fights without proper backup. Secure objectives like Lord to pressure lanes and force team fights in your favor.

emblems.emblem_desc: This setup is focused on high burst damage, allowing Chou to quickly eliminate priority targets. Rupture helps shred armor, Master Assassin increases damage in solo fights, and Lethal Ignition ensures he can finish off enemies with an extra damage boost. Ideal for aggressive players who rely on combos to secure kills.

equips.equip_desc: This build focuses on maximizing Chou's burst damage while ensuring he has the necessary sustain and durability to thrive in the EXP Lane. The combination of offensive and defensive items allows Chou to effectively engage, eliminate priority targets, and survive extended skirmishes.

hero.hero_overview: Chou is a powerful EXP lane fighter with high mobility, strong crowd control, and excellent 1v1 potential. He thrives in the solo lane by bullying weaker opponents, sustaining with spell vamp, and roaming when needed to assist teammates. With his "Jeet Kune Do" combos and "The Way of the Dragon" ultimate, he can isolate key enemies and secure crucial kills for his team.
hero.hero_strength:
Strong Early Game: Can dominate most melee heroes in 1v1 fights.

High Mobility: "Shunpo" provides quick repositioning and CC immunity.

Powerful Knockback: "The Way of the Dragon" can single out enemies for easy elimination.

Good Tower Diver: Can use his skills to escape after diving.

Counters Many Fighters: Can easily outplay heroes that rely on basic attacks.
hero.hero_weakness:
Skill Dependent: Requires proper combos to deal effective damage.

Weak Against Hard CC: Can be locked down and killed if not careful.

Item Dependent: Needs items like Blade of Heptaseas or Endless Battle to maximize damage.

Hard to Master: Requires precise timing and game sense.

data.recommend: Chou excels in mobility, crowd control, and 1v1 fights.

spell.spell_desc: Flicker is great for EXP Lane Chou as it improves mobility, allowing him to engage, escape, or reposition. It helps extend his ultimate range, surprise enemies, or escape fights. Chou can use Flicker to kick enemies into his turret or out of position, making him more effective in fights.

data.title: Chou(Exp lane)
